<!-- GENERATED by `mate docs gen` — do not hand-edit. Regenerate from the SSOT source. -->

# Gates reference

The gates the `check` ceiling runs — and how each one is kept honest.

| Gate | Teeth | Where |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline-lint` | script | `scripts/baseline-lint-test.sh` |
| `bump-check` | inline | the gate IS the test — bump-version-test.sh exercises scripts/bump-version.sh against a fixture tree, both axes. |
| `capabilities-lint` | script | `scripts/capabilities-lint-test.sh` |
| `citation-lint` | script | `scripts/citation-lint-test.sh` |
| `docs-check` | go | `internal/docs` |
| `doctrine-lint` | script | `scripts/doctrine-lint-test.sh` |
| `e2e` | inline | the fixtures live in the gate's own body: e2e.sh sabotages a pulled tree (drift, prune, template, paths seam) and asserts each red. |
| `fmt-check` | exempt | §0.1 — gofmt IS the oracle: the formatter defines the invariant, so a test of it would test gofmt, not mate. |
| `gate-of-gates` | go | `internal/gates` |
| `gen-check` | script | `scripts/gen-check-test.sh` |
| `link-check` | script | `scripts/link-check-test.sh` |
| `live-load-check` | flag | `--teeth` |
| `models-lint` | script | `scripts/models-lint-test.sh` |
| `portability-lint` | script | `scripts/portability-lint-test.sh` |
| `provider-agnostic` | script | `scripts/provider-agnostic-test.sh` |
| `registry-lint` | go | `internal/registry` |
| `rule-budget` | script | `scripts/rule-budget-test.sh` |
| `rule-router` | script | `scripts/rule-router-test.sh` |
| `self-harness-check` | script | `scripts/self-harness-check-test.sh` |
| `ssot-only-lint` | script | `scripts/ssot-only-lint-test.sh` |
| `surface-freshness` | script | `scripts/surface-freshness-test.sh` |
| `test` | inline | the Go suite IS the teeth — 23 _test.go files across the engine; a test of the tests is a regress. |
| `validator-backlog-lint` | script | `scripts/validator-backlog-lint-test.sh` |
| `vet` | exempt | §0.1 — the compiler/vet is the oracle; gating what the toolchain already guarantees is the noise that trains red-blindness. |

Teeth kinds: `script` (a teeth-test the gate's recipe runs) · `go` (the package's `*_test.go`) · `flag` (the gate's own sabotage mode) · `inline` (the gate IS a test suite) · `exempt` (§0.1 — the tool is its own oracle; allowed for `fmt-check` + `vet` only, enforced in code).

The `gate-of-gates` holds this table and the Makefile in agreement, both directions. It proves a teeth-test EXISTS and RUNS — it cannot prove the teeth BITE; that judgment stays with `/mate-validator new` step 5.
